text.title: security-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for security-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent widens allowlists or suppresses SAST rules to make findings go away
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Suppression hides real vulnerabilities. Lock .semgrepignore and .snyk behind a separate review path; require a one-line rationale and review-by date on every suppression.

failure-mode.description: Agent writes LLM-generated regex for input validation
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM-generated regex is famously bypassable. Use battle-tested libraries (bleach, defusedxml, validators, email-validator) and pin versions.

failure-mode.description: Agent auto-merges Dependabot/Snyk PRs without integration tests
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dependency updates break prod weekly without a green build + integration test gate on the changed dependency.

failure-mode.description: Agent hallucinates CVE IDs and remediation steps from training data
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always cross-check findings against NVD (nvd.nist.gov) or osv.dev before acting.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates crypto primitives ("here's a custom AES-GCM wrapper")
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Custom crypto is the most dangerous code class. Force cryptography.fernet, libsodium, or KMS-managed keys. Reject ad-hoc primitives.

failure-mode.description: Agent runs DAST automation against production URLs
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DAST scripts that mutate prod data cause real side effects. Restrict to non-prod URLs in CI policy and by ACL.

failure-mode.description: Agent suppresses CodeQL/Semgrep with `// nosec` without a rationale
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI must lint commits for naked suppressions. Every suppression requires a one-line reason.

failure-mode.description: Secret rotation requires only one of three coordinated changes (key in vault, app config, dependent services)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An agent doing only one of three creates an outage. Stage rotation in a runbook the agent follows step-by-step.
